original_prompt:
Hey there! I'm looking to head to Paris from May 10th to May 13th and I need a hotel recommendation. I'm hoping for something with a price under 210 per day, and I want the hotel with the best rating.
Also, I'm on the hunt for the best French cuisine restaurant for lunch on Sunday. Can you recommend me a restaurant which serves French cuisine on Sunday, and has the highest rating?
Can you give me an estimate of the minimum cost for the trip? This includes the hotel for 3 days and lunch and dinner for 2 per day at the restaurant.
Looking forward to hearing your recommendations! Tell me the hotel name and the restaurant name. Thanks in advance.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt gives a clear overall goal (hotel in Paris under 210/day with best rating; best French restaurant open on Sunday; estimate minimum trip cost), but it omits several important specifics that affect selection and cost calculation. Key ambiguities: (1) Currency is not specified for the 210/day price. (2) Dates lack a year, so whether a Sunday falls within May 10–13 is unclear. (3) “Under 210 per day” doesn’t define whether to compare to price_min or price_max, or whether equality (210) is allowed. (4) “Best rating” has no tie-breaking rule if multiple entries share the top rating. (5) The phrase “lunch and dinner for 2 per day” is ambiguous: it could mean two people per day (2 people × 2 meals) or two meals per day for one person (lunch + dinner). (6) Restaurant price semantics aren’t defined (price per person per meal vs. per meal aggregate). These missing details are material to the exact dataset queries and the minimum-cost computation, so the agent would need to make assumptions or ask follow-ups to be certain. Overall, the prompt provides the general goal but is missing key constraints — Highly Ambiguous.

variations.L1: I'm going to Paris in May and need a hotel and a good French place for Sunday lunch. Prefer hotels under 210 per day and want the best-rated option. Also give me an estimate of the minimum cost for the trip including hotel for 3 days and lunch + dinner for 2 people per day at the restaurant. Tell me the hotel and restaurant names.
variations.L2: Hey there! I'm looking to head to Paris from May 10th to May 13th and I need a hotel recommendation. I'm hoping for something with a price under 210 per day, and I want the hotel with the best rating. Also, I'm on the hunt for the best French cuisine restaurant for lunch on Sunday. Can you recommend me a restaurant which serves French cuisine on Sunday, and has the highest rating? Can you give me an estimate of the minimum cost for the trip? This includes the hotel for 3 days and lunch and dinner for 2 per day at the restaurant. Looking forward to hearing your recommendations! Tell me the hotel name and the restaurant name. Thanks in advance.
variations.L3: I'm traveling to Paris from May 10 to May 13 (3 nights). Please pick a hotel in Paris that costs less than 210 per night and has the highest rating among those within that price. Also recommend the highest-rated French restaurant that serves lunch on Sunday (open on Sundays) — I need it for Sunday lunch. For cost estimation, calculate a minimum trip cost using the hotel's lowest available nightly rate for the 3 nights and the restaurant's price per person for lunch and dinner for 2 people per day (2 people × 2 meals × 3 days). Provide the hotel and restaurant names, their ratings, and the minimum cost number.
variations.L4: Clear task: Find a hotel in Paris for May 10–13 (3 nights) whose price is under 210 EUR per night and choose the one with the highest rating (if multiple, state them). Find a French-cuisine restaurant in Paris that is open for lunch on Sunday and has the highest rating. Return: hotel name, hotel address, hotel rating and nightly rate used (use the hotel's minimum available nightly rate); restaurant name, restaurant address, restaurant rating and price per person; and compute the minimum total cost = (hotel_min_nightly_rate × 3 nights) + (restaurant_price_per_person × 2 people × 3 days). Include the numeric minimum cost in your answer.
variations.L5: Follow these exact steps and return a short result containing the hotel name, restaurant name, and the numeric minimum cost. 1) Call get_all_hotels_in_city with city = Paris. 2) From those results, filter hotels with price_max < 210. 3) Call get_hotels_prices for those Paris hotels if needed, then call get_rating_reviews_for_hotels for the filtered hotels and select the hotel(s) with the maximum rating. Use the selected hotel's price_min as the nightly rate. 4) Call get_all_restaurants_in_city with city = Paris. 5) Call get_cuisine_type_for_restaurants and filter to cuisine_type = French. 6) Call check_restaurant_opening_hours and keep restaurants whose operating_hours include 'Sunday' (open for Sunday lunch). 7) Call get_rating_reviews_for_restaurants for the remaining restaurants and select the one(s) with the maximum rating. 8) For the chosen restaurant, call get_restaurants_address and get_price_for_restaurants to obtain its address and price per person. 9) Compute minimum_expense = selected_hotel.price_min * 3 + selected_restaurant.price_per_person * 2 * 3. 10) Return the hotel name, restaurant name, and the numeric minimum_expense value (and also include addresses and ratings).
